post_text: Kubernetes autoscaling traps can be devastating. We recently experienced a production incident where our cluster autoscaler failed to scale down, resulting in significant resource waste. The issue was caused by a misconfigured scale-down delay, which prevented the autoscaler from removing unnecessary nodes. Upon further investigation, we discovered that the root cause was a combination of incorrect resource quotas and inadequate monitoring. To resolve the issue, we implemented a more robust monitoring system using Prometheus and Grafana, and reconfigured our resource quotas to ensure more efficient scaling. We also established a regular review process to ensure our autoscaling configuration remains optimal. Key takeaways from this incident include the importance of proper resource quota configuration, adequate monitoring, and regular review of autoscaling settings. What strategies do you use to optimize your Kubernetes autoscaling configuration and prevent similar traps? #Kubernetes #DevSecOps #CloudSecurity #PlatformEngineering
